skill.name: youtube-batch-transcript-extractor-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube video transcripts and metadata in batch using the BrowserAct API. Provide keywords and filters to batch extract full transcripts, likes, and channel metadata without writing crawler scripts.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube video transcripts and metadata in batch via BrowserAct API
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill makes legitimate API calls to BrowserAct service for YouTube transcript extraction. Network access is limited to the BrowserAct API endpoint. The BROWSERACT_API_KEY is used for OAuth bearer token authentication, which is standard practice. External command execution is limited to running the provided Python script with user-supplied keywords. All static findings are false positives or legitimate usage patterns.

high_findings.title: Static Analyzer Misclassification: Bearer Token Auth Flagged as Weak Crypto
high_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 'Bearer {api_key}' (line 21) as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a false positive. The code uses standard OAuth 2.0 Bearer token authentication, which is a widely accepted authentication pattern. The 'Bearer' prefix is part of the HTTP Authorization header format, not a cryptographic algorithm.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly uses requests library with Bearer token auth pattern which is standard OAuth practice, not weak cryptography

high_findings.title: Static Analyzer Misclassification: API Key Documentation Flagged
high_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged references to BROWSERACT_API_KEY in SKILL.md as 'Generic API/secret keys'. This is a false positive. SKILL.md documents the requirement for users to obtain their own API key from BrowserAct. This is legitimate documentation of an external service dependency, not exposure of secrets.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md documents an environment variable name requirement, it does not expose actual secrets - standard skill documentation

medium_findings.title: Backtick Pattern Detection in Markdown Documentation
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick characters in SKILL.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. This is a false positive. The backticks are used for markdown code formatting in the documentation file, not actual shell command execution. The SKILL.md is a README-style document containing example commands for users to run.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown documentation are for code formatting, not execution. All flagged locations are in SKILL.md which contains example commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded BrowserAct API URLs. This is legitimate for an API integration skill. The URLs point to the official BrowserAct service endpoints and are not user-controlled or exploitable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded legitimate service endpoints are standard practice for API integration skills

low_findings.title: JSON Response Parsing Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged JSON response parsing (checking for 'id' key, parsing status) as 'system reconnaissance'. This is a false positive. The code is simply parsing the BrowserAct API JSON responses to extract task IDs and status values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API response parsing, not system reconnaissance. Task IDs are extracted from BrowserAct API responses

content.user_title: Extract YouTube transcripts in batch via API
content.value_statement: This skill automates YouTube transcript extraction at scale using the BrowserAct API. Users can batch extract full video transcripts, metadata, and engagement data without writing crawler scripts or handling CAPTCHAs.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube by keywords and extract transcript data for matching videos
Filter results by upload date (today, this week, this month, this year)
Batch extract transcripts from multiple videos in a single API call
Retrieve video metadata including title, URL, publisher, and like count
Process results through BrowserAct automated browser workflow without CAPTCHAs
Handle API authentication errors gracefully with retry logic
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key to function (free tier available at browseract.com)
Transcript availability depends on whether videos have closed captions enabled
Processing time may take several minutes for large batch requests
Rate limits apply based on BrowserAct API plan tier

use_cases.title: Research video content for AI summarization pipelines
use_cases.description: Extract transcripts from multiple YouTube videos to build training datasets or feed into AI summarization systems. Batch collect full video content without manual copying.

use_cases.title: Monitor trending content across topic areas
use_cases.description: Track recent YouTube videos about specific keywords or topics by extracting transcripts from videos published this week. Build media monitoring workflows.

use_cases.title: Build searchable transcript archives
use_cases.description: Collect transcripts from curated YouTube channels or video lists for offline search. Create transcript databases for knowledge management systems.

prompt_templates.title: Basic batch extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the YouTube batch transcript extractor to get transcripts for videos about [topic] published this week. Limit to [5] videos.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need quick transcript samples for a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Large-scale collection
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract transcripts from [20] YouTube videos about [topic] published this month. Collect video titles, URLs, publisher info, and like counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: When building a research dataset requires bulk transcript collection

prompt_templates.title: Filtered by timeframe
prompt_templates.prompt: Get transcripts for YouTube videos about [topic] from [this year]. Only include videos with at least basic transcript available.
prompt_templates.scenario: When focused on recent or historical content within a specific period

prompt_templates.title: Channel-specific extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for and extract transcripts from YouTube videos by [channel name] about [topic]. Get the full transcript and metadata for each video.
prompt_templates.scenario: When analyzing content from specific creators or channels

best_practices: Start with small limits (1-5 videos) to test the workflow before running large batches
Use specific keywords rather than broad terms to get more relevant transcript results
Check that your BrowserAct API key is valid before running large batch extractions
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to extract transcripts for copyright-infringing purposes or unauthorized content reproduction
Avoid requesting extremely large batches (100+ videos) as this may timeout or hit rate limits
Do not share extracted transcripts in ways that violate YouTube content creator terms of service

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct API key. Get one free at browseract.com/reception/integrations. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Why are some video transcripts missing?
faq.answer: Transcripts are only available when the video creator has enabled closed captions. Videos without captions cannot have transcripts extracted.

faq.question: How long does batch extraction take?
faq.answer: Processing time varies. Small batches (1-5 videos) typically complete in 2-5 minutes. Larger batches may take 10+ minutes due to automated browser workflow.

faq.question: What happens if the extraction fails?
faq.answer: The skill will automatically retry once if the failure is not due to invalid authorization. If the API key is invalid, it will prompt you to check your credentials.

faq.question: Can I filter videos by language?
faq.answer: This skill does not currently support language filtering. The BrowserAct API extracts whatever captions are available on each video.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of videos I can extract in one batch?
faq.answer: The default limit is 5 videos. You can increase this, but very large batches may timeout. BrowserAct rate limits also apply based on your API plan.
